_README: SOURCE OF TRUTH for ghcr.io/imazen container package NAMES used by the zen fleet/sweep infra. The rule: ONE ghcr package per artifact; variants are TAGS, never new package names. A new package name MAY ONLY be created by adding it to `packages[]` here in the same reviewed change — CI (`just ghcr-check`) fails if any active infra file references a `ghcr.io/imazen/<name>` that is not listed here. See docs/GHCR_PACKAGES.md for the policy, tag conventions, and the migration playbook for the `deprecated` names below.

packages.name: zenmetrics-sweep
packages.purpose: GPU/CPU codec-sweep + metric-score worker (the 'omni' image baking the zenmetrics CLI + all 5 codecs + GPU/CPU metrics). The image fleet boxes pull to encode and score.
packages.builds_from: Dockerfile.sweep.v27 (amd64) / scripts/sweep/Dockerfile.sweep.hetzner.v1 (arm64) — precompiled binary COPY'd in.
tag_convention: latest            — master HEAD, amd64
v<NN>             — image generation (v26, v27, ...); bump for a layering/base change, NOT a code push
v<NN>-<date>      — pinned immutable build of generation NN
hetzner | salad   — provider-specific variant (arch/entrypoint), e.g. :hetzner is the arm64 CAX build
split             — the encode-once/score-many SPLIT worker variant
<short-sha>       — exact-commit pin

packages.name: zenfleet-worker
packages.purpose: Job-system fleet worker AND the shared bake-everything BASE (via base-* tags) that every Rust leaf image (zenmetrics-sweep, zen-train, the exec variants) FROMs. The runnable worker tags (latest/exec/exec-gpu) carry the worker binary; the base-* tags carry ONLY the stable deps every image needs to TRAIN or RUN — OS, R2 tooling (aws-cli v2 + s5cmd), python3 + pyarrow/numpy, jq, build tools, and (the cuda base) the CUDA 12.6 runtime + cuda_dlsym_stub. No pytorch (that stays in pycvvdp-scorer); no Rust toolchain (binaries are built in CI and COPY'd in). Folding the base in here (vs a separate package) keeps the org to ONE worker-family package.
packages.builds_from: runnable worker tags: .github/workflows/jobworker-image.yml -> crates/zenfleet-worker/Dockerfile. base-* tags: .github/workflows/base-image.yml -> docker/base/Dockerfile (multi-stage common -> nocuda/cuda), native runners.
tag_convention: latest            — master HEAD multi-arch manifest (worker binary)
<short-sha>       — every push (worker binary)
exec              — worker base + zenmetrics jobexec real executor (CPU)
exec-gpu          — exec variant on a CUDA base for GPU metric scoring
base-x86          — FROM-able base layer, amd64, no CUDA (CPU encode/score, training-data prep)
base-arm          — FROM-able base layer, arm64, no CUDA (Hetzner CAX / Oracle ARM tiers)
base-x86-cuda     — base + CUDA 12.6 runtime + cuda_dlsym_stub, amd64 (GPU metrics, cubecl-cuda training)
base-<variant>-<short-sha> — exact-commit immutable pin of a base tag
buildcache-* — buildkit registry cache (not a runnable image)

packages.name: pycvvdp-scorer
packages.purpose: pycvvdp (PyTorch) CVVDP reference scorer. Kept a SEPARATE package on purpose: it carries ~3 GB of torch, so it must not bloat the cold-start of the sweep image.
tag_convention: latest            — current scorer
<pycvvdp-version> — e.g. 0.5.4, pinning the upstream pycvvdp release

packages.name: zen-train
packages.purpose: Standalone trainer(s) that consume parquet metric/feature sidecars and bake ZNPR picker/metric models. The `latest`/burn tags are the burn-ranknet trainer (its own cubecl graph; never shares a cargo graph with the GPU-metric crates — see CLAUDE.md 'burn'). The `hybrid-cpu` tag is the per-codec dual-model picker trainer (picker_tree_ab A/B + train_hybrid teacher/student) for the Hetzner one-box-per-codec fleet (scripts/train/).
packages.builds_from: burn tags: the burn-ranknet trainer (separate binary). hybrid-cpu tag: scripts/train/Dockerfile.hybrid-cpu via scripts/train/build_hybrid_image.sh (FROM zenfleet-worker:base-x86; torch-CPU+sklearn+pandas baked; precompiled picker_tree_ab + zenpredict-bake COPY'd in).
tag_convention: latest            — current burn trainer
hybrid-cpu        — per-codec dual-model picker trainer (CPU; torch+sklearn+pandas + the Rust bins)
<short-sha>       — exact-commit pin

deprecated._README: Splinter package names that ALREADY EXIST and are GRANDFATHERED: `just ghcr-check` WARNS (does not fail) when these appear in active infra so the live fleet keeps working, and tolerates them in historical docs. `just ghcr-check --strict` FAILS on any of them in infra — flip to strict once migration is done. Each maps to the canonical `name[:tag]` it should become. Migration playbook: docs/GHCR_PACKAGES.md. The end-state is this map EMPTY.
